publication.title: Kungfu: The Session Is Not the Work
publication.abstract: Verified continuity for Agent work that outlives the chat.

sectionMap.title: Executive Summary: The Work Must Outlive the Chat
subsections: One Product Decision, Two Adoption Paths
sectionMap.summary: The session is the right unit of interaction—and the wrong unit of continuity for long-running Agent work. A session can host valuable code, research, plans, operations, and decisions. It cannot, by itself, authoritatively settle what the project has become, what was accepted, which authority still applies, or where another participant may safely continue. When the session closes, a transcript, summary, or unverified memory is not enough. The model may be capable while the work remains fragile.

sectionMap.title: The Continuity Gap
sectionMap.summary: Real work is not a prompt-response pair. It has accepted state, causal history, direction, authority, evidence, unresolved conflict, and consequences. It may cross context windows, models, devices, repositories, organizations, and years. A session is only one observer's temporary projection of that work.

sectionMap.title: The Strategic Choice for Agent Builders
subsections: What Embedding Does and Does Not Mean
sectionMap.summary: An Agent vendor already owns the scarce product assets: the interface, model experience, accounts, permissions, policies, cloud, distribution, and customer relationship. It should preserve those assets. The strategic question is not whether to become a Kungfu-branded application. It is whether to spend years reconstructing a durable runtime before its users can keep work across chats.

sectionMap.title: Project Cut: The First User Object
subsections: A Settlement, Not a Fourth Fact Engine
sectionMap.summary: The first user layer in Kungfu is not a log viewer, model selector, or internal Fact table. It is the Project Cut: the highest ordinary interface through which a person or Agent inspects what a project has formally become and from where it may continue. This product decision keeps rigor below the surface while giving the user one actionable coordinate above it.

sectionMap.title: How Work Continues Across Sessions
sectionMap.summary: Continuity is a closed loop, not a saved transcript. Kungfu's target product loop is:

sectionMap.title: The Runtime Beneath the Cut
subsections: Buildchain Is Release Evidence, Not Runtime State
sectionMap.summary: Project Cut is the first user object, but it can be trusted only because its inputs and lifecycle remain verifiable beneath the product surface. libkungfu is the reference implementation of that runtime substrate. It owns how local Facts and Episodes are recorded, ordered, stored, queried, verified, exported, and recovered while the embedding application owns its domain facts and product decisions.

sectionMap.title: KFD and Independent Agent Hubs
sectionMap.summary: KFD—Kung Fu Decisions—is an open engineering standard for a small, testable foundation for reliable action and continuity under uncertainty. It is a canonical public decision registry, not a Kungfu product API, a central service, a universal workflow language, or a belief test. Products, organizations, humans, Agents, and other bounded systems can adopt exact decisions within a declared scope and publish their own mappings, evidence, qualification, and residual risk.

sectionMap.title: Authority, Trust, and Evidence
sectionMap.summary: Continuity is unsafe when a system collapses transport, identity, occurrence, judgment, and authority into a single success flag. Kungfu and KFD preserve the separations that keep responsibility attributable.

sectionMap.title: Current State, Adoption, and Roadmap
subsections: The Builder Path: Inspect, Embed, Prove, Connect
sectionMap.summary: This paper is an alpha positioning and architecture document. It recommends immediate implementation work while keeping capability claims below the public evidence ceiling.

sectionMap.title: Conclusion
sectionMap.summary: The market is moving from Agents that answer inside a chat to Agents that carry responsibility across real work. The decisive product question is no longer only how intelligent one session can be. It is whether the work remains verifiable, recoverable, and continuable after that session disappears.

references.title: Buildchain Release Evidence
references.author: Kungfu Origin Technology Limited
references.note: Artifact, package, source, publication, and release provenance; not runtime or project authority

verification.residualRisk: This is an alpha product and architecture paper based on first-party evidence; it does not establish external vendor adoption, certification, stable Hub interoperability, or broad production readiness.

agentSupplyChain.categoryStatement: Kungfu is an open Agent Supply Chain protocol stack for discovering how Agent products cooperate, binding claims to exact software artifacts, establishing purpose-bound trust, preserving durable work facts, and carrying that work across independently owned Hubs.
agentSupplyChain.claimBoundary: Kungfu does not claim that a multi-Hub ecosystem already exists. It proves that Agent discovery, software provenance, trust, durable work state, and portability no longer need to be rebuilt or locked inside each Hub.

layers.input: Product-owned value, constraints, choices, commands, Exit, and record declarations
layers.output: A stable human-and-agent discovery surface for bounded cooperation
layers.statement: Discover how products cooperate through inspectable value, constraints, choices, commands, Exit, and records.
knownLimits: KFD-3 discovery is inspectable product guidance, not a hidden prompt or forced adoption mechanism.
The current evidence proves the KFD alpha surface, not external vendor adoption or stable certification.

layers.input: KFD-3-discoverable product declarations and an exact source cut
layers.output: Artifact-bound provenance, checks, and promotion evidence
layers.statement: Bind product-owned declarations to exact source, build, artifact, checks, and promotion evidence.
knownLimits: Buildchain does not create product facts or make the receiver's trust decision.
A passing Passport proves an exact release relation, not blanket certification, safety, or adoption.

layers.input: Exact-artifact evidence, a declared purpose, and receiver policy
layers.output: A purpose-bound assessment with residual risk and decision ownership
layers.statement: Assess claims for a declared purpose while retaining residual risk and decision ownership.
knownLimits: KFD-2 is purpose-, cut-, and evidence-bound; it is not a company reputation score or universal trust certificate.
A prior assessment does not authorize a changed artifact, purpose, receiver policy, or future release.

layers.input: Receiver-admitted work facts, commands, Episodes, and roots
layers.output: Ordered durable records, export, recovery, and qualification evidence
layers.statement: Preserve admitted work facts, Episodes, roots, export, and recovery evidence while applications own domain facts.
knownLimits: Applications retain authority over domain facts; libkungfu owns admitted runtime records and ordering within its declared boundary.
JSON is an edge projection, not a second authoritative data plane, and qualification remains platform- and cut-specific.

layers.input: Bounded responsibility objects with rooted evidence and explicit authority
layers.output: Portable envelopes, conformance results, and receiver-owned admission decisions
layers.statement: Carry bounded responsibility objects across independently owned products with receiver-owned admission.
knownLimits: The public profile enables independent implementations but does not prove a second independent production Hub.
Portability preserves bounded roots, verdicts, conflicts, commitments, and digests; it does not promise lossless one-click migration.

notClaimed: universal trust or blanket stable compatibility
agentSupplyChain.vendorNextAction: Assign a technical and product owner, run a bounded 30-day assessment, build one adapter or conformance spike, submit protocol gaps, then decide to adopt, co-shape, or monitor.
